name: Overnight Souffle
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a sauté pan over medium heat.
2. Cook mushrooms and onions for two minutes or until just slightly tender. Remove with a slotted spoon.
3. Dip eight bread slices into the pan to butter one side. Place bread, butter side down, in an oblong casserole (13 x 9 x 2 inches).
4. Layer half of the mushrooms and onions, bacon, celery, and chilies on top of the bread.
5. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and one tablespoon of dill.
6. Repeat the bread layer and the remaining half of ingredients as above, but add tomato slices before cheese.
7. Thoroughly mix eggs, cream, and mustard in a separate bowl.
8. Pour the egg mixture over the casserole.
9. Cover with waxed paper and refrigerate overnight.
10. Before baking, allow the casserole to stand at room temperature for at least 30 minutes.
11. Preheat oven to 350°F.
12. Bake the casserole uncovered for 45 minutes or until a soufflé-like consistency is achieved.
13. Remove from oven and let cool for five minutes before slicing.
14. Leftovers may be frozen.
